metcons.name: Quick Spark

stimulus.feel: Short sprint. Hang cleans should be fast touch-and-go. Chest-to-bar pull-ups in quick sets or unbroken.
stimulus.intent: Barbell cycling speed, pulling under fatigue, short-duration intensity

metcons.coachNotes: Touch-and-go hang cleans if possible. C2B should be unbroken in round 1. If you're grinding singles on the bar from the start, go lighter. This should be a sprint, not a grind.

metcons.name: Sweet Breeze

stimulus.feel: A true engine test. Find a pace you can hold for 20 minutes. The row sets the rhythm, the swings keep you honest, and the toes-to-bar test your midline under fatigue.
stimulus.intent: Aerobic capacity, pacing discipline, mixed modal endurance

metcons.coachNotes: This is a 20-minute pacer, not a sprint. Athletes should find their rhythm by round 3 and hold it. KB swings unbroken every round. TTB in 1-2 sets. Row at a sustainable pace. Target 7-10 rounds at Rx.

metcons.name: Heavy Slate

stimulus.feel: Heavy barbell complex with running. Low reps but challenging loads. The 200m between rounds stops you from resting too long. Push-ups accumulate under shoulder fatigue.
stimulus.intent: Strength endurance across barbell movements, pacing heavy loads with bodyweight volume

metcons.coachNotes: The run should be a jog, not a sprint. Save energy for the barbell. Same bar for both front squat and push press. Front squats from the floor (clean first rep each round). Push-ups should stay unbroken through at least round 3. Scale load to keep all movements unbroken every round.

metcons.name: Sharp Cedar

stimulus.feel: Interval work. Push hard, recover within the minute. Cleans should be crisp, burpees are the engine piece. Should have 15-20s rest each round at Rx.
stimulus.intent: Power output under repeating time pressure, barbell skill under fatigue

metcons.coachNotes: Squat cleans should be crisp singles or quick touch-and-go. No grinding reps. If athletes can't finish within 40-45 seconds, reduce load or reps. The rest within each minute matters.

metcons.name: Spicy Flint

stimulus.feel: High intensity couplet. The descending reps give you hope, but the deadlifts get heavier as your grip fades. Burpees are relentless. Should hurt from round 2 onward.
stimulus.intent: High-intensity hip hinge under fatigue, mental toughness through descending reps

metcons.coachNotes: Deadlifts should be touch-and-go for the round of 21 at Rx. If you're dropping every rep from the start, go lighter. Burpees set the pace. Just keep moving.

metcons.name: Long Tide

stimulus.feel: Steady engine work. No single movement should gas you. Find a rhythm and hold it for 25 minutes. The snatches are the separator.
stimulus.intent: Aerobic capacity, pacing discipline, sustained mixed modal output

metcons.coachNotes: This is a pacer, not a sprint. Athletes should be able to talk between movements. If the snatches slow to grinding singles, go lighter. Run should be conversational pace. Wall balls unbroken. Target 4-6 rounds at Rx.

metcons.name: Dark Granite

stimulus.feel: Gymnastics grinder. Four rounds means you need to pace the handstand push-ups from round 1. Pistols demand control. Toes-to-bar test your grip and midline when everything is tired.
stimulus.intent: Gymnastics skill under sustained fatigue, unilateral strength, coordination

metcons.coachNotes: HSPU in small sets from round 1. Don't burn out early. Pistols controlled, not rushed. TTB are where most athletes slow down. Scale to maintain quality movement across all 4 rounds. Target consistent round splits.

metcons.name: Light Moss

stimulus.feel: Bodyweight grinder. Two rounds of the descending chipper. No equipment, no excuses. The second round is where character shows.
stimulus.intent: Bodyweight conditioning, sustained effort, mental toughness through repeated volume

metcons.coachNotes: Round 1 should feel manageable. Don't go out too hot. Round 2 starts right back at 50 DU, which is a mental challenge. Jumping lunges are where most athletes slow down. Scale to keep moving continuously through both rounds.

metcons.name: Thick Chalk

stimulus.feel: Classic mixed modal grind with running. The 400m gives you a moment to breathe before hitting the thrusters. Your legs won't thank you after the box jumps. Find a pace and hold it.
stimulus.intent: Mixed modal capacity, barbell cycling under fatigue, pacing with running

metcons.coachNotes: Use the run to recover, not to sprint. Thrusters in sets of 5-8. Don't go to failure in round 1. Box jumps steady and controlled. Scale load to keep moving through all 3 rounds.

metcons.name: Loud Steel

stimulus.feel: Big and explosive. The rows bookend the barbell work. You'll need to find your pace on the erg knowing 30 clean and jerks are waiting. The second row is where mental toughness shows.
stimulus.intent: Engine capacity, explosive barbell cycling, pacing strategy

metcons.coachNotes: Don't blow up on the first row. Save energy for the barbell. C&J should be quick singles or small touch-and-go sets. The second row will feel harder than the first. That's normal. Settle in and hold a pace.

metcons.name: Sweet Rust

stimulus.feel: Balanced pressing and pulling with running. Push press should be touch-and-go. Pull-ups in quick sets. The run is active recovery.
stimulus.intent: Upper body push/pull endurance, pacing with running

metcons.coachNotes: Push press unbroken through round 2 at least. Same pacing every round. If you're resting more than 5 seconds between movements, go lighter.

metcons.name: Quick Birch

stimulus.feel: Fast sprint. Row sets the pace, swings should be unbroken, burpees just keep moving. Transitions matter.
stimulus.intent: Short-duration power output, fast transitions

metcons.coachNotes: Row hard but don't empty the tank. KB swings unbroken every round. Burpees are the separator. Target sub-8 at Rx.

metcons.name: Long Copper

stimulus.feel: Long grinder. Five movements per round means constant transitions. No single movement should break you. The deadlifts accumulate over 30 minutes.
stimulus.intent: Aerobic capacity, pacing across multiple modalities, sustained output

metcons.coachNotes: This is a 30-minute test. Find your pace by round 2 and hold it. Wall balls unbroken. Deadlifts should be quick touch-and-go. Target 5-7 rounds at Rx.

metcons.name: Thick Iron

stimulus.feel: Heavy posterior chain grind. The run gives you time to breathe before the next round of deadlifts. Toes-to-bar test your grip after heavy pulling.
stimulus.intent: Posterior chain endurance, midline under fatigue, pacing heavy loads

metcons.coachNotes: Deadlifts should be touch-and-go in sets of 5 at minimum. If you're doing singles from the start, go lighter. TTB will get harder each round. Scale to keep quality.

metcons.name: Spicy Gravel

stimulus.feel: Chipper. Each piece builds on the last. The power cleans in the middle are the separator. Second set of wall balls feels different from the first.
stimulus.intent: Sustained output across varied movements, mental toughness through a long chipper

metcons.coachNotes: Don't sprint the first row. Wall balls should be unbroken. Power cleans in sets of 5 at minimum. The second row will feel harder. That's expected.

metcons.name: Sharp Frost

stimulus.feel: Interval work. Thruster minutes are the grind, toes-to-bar minutes test your midline. Should have 15-20s rest each minute at Rx.
stimulus.intent: Pressing power and midline stability under interval fatigue

metcons.coachNotes: Thrusters should be unbroken every round. If you can't finish in 40 seconds, reduce load or reps. TTB in 1-2 quick sets.

metcons.name: Heavy Brick

stimulus.feel: Heavy and long. The row sets the tempo. Front squats are the challenge. C2B pull-ups test your grip when your legs are shot. The short run at the end flushes the legs before the next round.
stimulus.intent: Strength endurance, heavy barbell under fatigue, mixed modal capacity

metcons.coachNotes: Row at 2:00/500m pace. Front squats from the floor (clean first rep). Break into sets of 4-6 if needed. C2B in quick sets. The run should be a recovery jog.

metcons.name: Sweet Ash

stimulus.feel: Balanced 20-minute effort. The run anchors each round. Power snatches reward good technique under fatigue. Box jumps keep the heart rate up.
stimulus.intent: Mixed modal capacity, Olympic lifting skill under fatigue, pacing

metcons.coachNotes: Run at 80% effort. Power snatches should be crisp. No grinding reps. If technique breaks down, go lighter. Box jumps steady and controlled. Target 5-7 rounds at Rx.

metcons.name: Dark Ember

stimulus.feel: Pulling and hinging grinder. C2B pull-ups tax the grip, KB swings tax the posterior chain, burpees are the engine. Four rounds means you need to pace.
stimulus.intent: Pulling endurance, hip hinge capacity, bodyweight conditioning

metcons.coachNotes: C2B in sets of 3-5. KB swings unbroken every round. Burpees are where you can push the pace. Scale to maintain quality C2B across all 4 rounds.

metcons.name: Quick Salt

stimulus.feel: Classic couplet sprint. The descending reps give you hope. Thrusters burn the legs, pull-ups burn the grip. Go fast or go home.
stimulus.intent: High-intensity sprint, thruster cycling, pulling under fatigue

metcons.coachNotes: Thrusters unbroken in the round of 21 if possible. Pull-ups in big sets. This should be a sprint. If you're resting more than 10 seconds between movements, go lighter.

metcons.name: Sweet Storm

team.description: Alternate full rounds. One partner works while the other rests. Each partner completes 4 rounds.

stimulus.feel: Steady partner grind. Wall balls and running set the pace. TTB should be quick sets. Rest while your partner works, but stay warm.
stimulus.intent: Mixed modal endurance with a partner. Pacing and transitions matter.

metcons.coachNotes: Each partner does 4 rounds. The rest between rounds should keep you fresh enough to push the pace. Wall balls unbroken every round. TTB in 1-2 sets max. Run should be a consistent effort, not a sprint.

metcons.name: Loud Waves

team.description: Alternate full rounds. One partner works while the other rests. Score is total rounds + reps for the team.

stimulus.feel: Moderate pace partner AMRAP. The cleans should be touch-and-go, box jumps fast. Row is recovery. Push the pace knowing you have built-in rest.
stimulus.intent: Barbell cycling and explosiveness with aerobic base, partner format

metcons.coachNotes: Each partner should aim for similar round times. HPC should be unbroken or fast doubles. Box jumps controlled but quick. Row is active recovery. Target 10-14 total rounds at Rx.

metcons.name: Thick Cloud

team.description: Split the total work between partners however you choose. Only one partner works at a time.

stimulus.feel: High-volume partner chipper. Break up the work strategically. KB swings and wall balls should stay in big sets. Burpees are the grind. Row is the finisher.
stimulus.intent: Volume tolerance, work distribution strategy, mixed modal endurance

metcons.coachNotes: Partition however you want. Common strategy: alternate every 20-25 reps on wall balls and KB swings, every 10-15 on burpees. One person rows while the other rests. Total reps shown are for the TEAM.

metcons.name: Spicy Thunder

team.description: Alternate full rounds. One partner works while the other rests. Each partner completes 5 rounds.

stimulus.feel: Fast barbell cycling with a push. Cleans should be crisp and fast. Push-ups are the recovery movement. Short rounds, high intensity.
stimulus.intent: Power output and barbell cycling speed in a team setting

metcons.coachNotes: Quick rounds. Power cleans should be touch-and-go or fast singles. Push-ups unbroken every round. Each round should take 30-50 seconds. Use the rest between rounds to recover and push hard.

metcons.name: Heavy River

team.description: Split the total work between partners however you choose. Only one partner works at a time on the barbell. Both can run simultaneously.

stimulus.feel: Heavy barbell chipper with running. The deadlifts and cleans are the grind. Double-unders test coordination under fatigue. Running is transition and recovery.
stimulus.intent: Strength endurance with barbell cycling, team work distribution

metcons.coachNotes: Total reps shown are for the TEAM. Each partner uses their own bar at their own weight. Common split: alternate every 10 reps on barbell movements. DUs can be split however. Run together or alternate laps.

metcons.name: Sharp Gale

team.description: Alternate full rounds. One partner works while the other rests. Score is total rounds + reps for the team.

stimulus.feel: Balanced partner AMRAP. Thrusters are the grind, pull-ups test gymnastics under fatigue, row is pacing. Push knowing your partner is resting.
stimulus.intent: Mixed modal capacity in a team format, pacing discipline

metcons.coachNotes: Thrusters should be unbroken or quick sets of 4. Pull-ups in 1-2 sets. Row at a strong but sustainable pace. Each round should take 1:30-2:00 at Rx. Target 12-16 total rounds.

metcons.name: Dark Ridge

stimulus.feel: Gymnastics grind. TTB and HSPU are the skill pieces. Running keeps the heart rate up. Should feel technical, not just cardio.
stimulus.intent: Gymnastics skill under fatigue, midline and pressing endurance

metcons.coachNotes: TTB in sets of 4-6 at Rx. HSPU strict or kipping, whatever keeps you moving. Scale HSPU aggressively to keep the workout under the cap. Run is recovery pace.

metcons.name: Sweet Willow

stimulus.feel: Balanced mixed modal. KB swings set the pace, box jumps keep you explosive, C2B test your pulling. Should feel sustainable with consistent round times.
stimulus.intent: Mixed modal conditioning, gymnastics pulling under moderate fatigue

metcons.coachNotes: KB swings unbroken every round. Box jumps controlled but quick. C2B in 1-2 sets. Find your rhythm by round 3 and hold it. Target 7-10 rounds at Rx.

metcons.name: Light Feather

stimulus.feel: Bodyweight sprint. Push-ups and air squats should be unbroken. Burpees are the limiter. Running keeps the heart rate pegged. Fast and simple.
stimulus.intent: Bodyweight conditioning, high turnover, minimal equipment

metcons.coachNotes: No barbell, no excuses. Push-ups and air squats unbroken every round. Burpees steady pace, just keep moving. Run at 80% effort. Should feel like a sprint, not a grind.

metcons.name: Dark Vine

stimulus.feel: E2MOM gymnastics intervals. Each 2-minute window has two movements. Work fast, rest within the window. Should have 30-40s rest each round.
stimulus.intent: Gymnastics skill under interval pressure, coordination and midline

metcons.coachNotes: E2MOM: 8 rounds alternating A/B (4 of each). Double-unders first to get the heart rate up, then gymnastics. If you can't finish in 80-90 seconds, scale the DU count.

metcons.name: Long Meadow

stimulus.feel: Sunday easy engine. This is a conversation-pace workout. No movement should gas you. Find a rhythm and hold it for 30 minutes. Active recovery day.
stimulus.intent: Aerobic base building, active recovery, low-intensity steady state

metcons.coachNotes: Truly easy pace. You should be able to talk between movements. Run at 60-70% effort. Push-ups and air squats steady and unbroken. Row at a comfortable pace. This is about moving, not competing.

metcons.name: Sweet Pebble

stimulus.feel: Easy Sunday grind. Low skill, moderate volume. Lunges and sit-ups are the work. Running and ring rows keep things moving. Conversation pace throughout.
stimulus.intent: Active recovery, bodyweight volume, low-intensity movement

metcons.coachNotes: Easy pace. Walking lunges controlled and even. Sit-ups at a steady rhythm. Ring rows with good body position. Run is a jog, not a run. No movement should feel hard.

metcons.name: Light Dew

stimulus.feel: Light Sunday engine. Row sets the pace. KB swings and step-ups are low-skill, low-impact. Easy day, just move for 25 minutes.
stimulus.intent: Low-intensity aerobic work, active recovery with minimal impact

metcons.coachNotes: Easy and steady. Row at conversation pace. KB swings should feel light and easy. Box step-ups controlled, no jumping. This is about blood flow and recovery, not performance.

metcons.name: Long Drift

stimulus.feel: Long bodyweight Sunday. Running is the backbone. Air squats, sit-ups, and push-ups fill the rounds. Easy, steady, no equipment needed.
stimulus.intent: Active recovery, aerobic volume, bodyweight endurance

metcons.coachNotes: Jog the 400m. Everything else at an easy, steady pace. Air squats unbroken. Sit-ups rhythmic. Push-ups in whatever sets keep you moving. No rushing, no resting. Just move.
